briefdescription.para: Invalid draw command type.
Arbitrary path draw command type.
Circle draw command type.
Arbitrary path drawn with sub-pixel precision (1/8th precision)

Draw commands are the basic building block of the draw command system, encoding the type of command to draw, the stroke width and color, fill color, and points that define the path (or center of a circle.

Draw command frames contain a list of commands to draw for that frame and a duration, indicating the length of time for which the frame should be drawn in an animation sequence. Frames form the building blocks of a GDrawCommandSequence, which consists of multiple frames.
Draw command images contain a list of commands that can be drawn. An image can be loaded from PDC file data.
Draw command lists contain a list of commands that can be iterated over and drawn all at once.

memberdef.definition: typedef bool(* GDrawCommandListIteratorCb) (GDrawCommand *command, uint32_t index, void *context)
memberdef.name: GDrawCommandListIteratorCb

briefdescription.para: Callback for iterating over draw command list.

parameterdescription.para: current GDrawCommand in iteration
index of the current command in the list
context pointer for the iteration operation

simplesect.para: true if the iteration should continue after this command is processed

briefdescription.para: Draw command sequences allow the animation of frames over time. Each sequence has a list of frames that can be accessed by the elapsed duration of the animation (not maintained internally) or by index. Sequences can be loaded from PDC file data.
Draw a command.

parameterdescription.para: The destination graphics context in which to draw
GDrawCommand to draw

briefdescription.para: Draw a frame.

parameterdescription.para: The destination graphics context in which to draw
The sequence from which the frame comes from (this is required)
Frame to draw
Offset from draw context origin to draw the frame

briefdescription.para: Get the command list of the frame.

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommandFrame from which to get the command list

simplesect.para: command list

briefdescription.para: Get the duration of the frame.

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommandFrame from which to get the duration

simplesect.para: duration of the frame in milliseconds

briefdescription.para: Set the duration of the frame.

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommandFrame for which to set the duration
duration of the frame in milliseconds

briefdescription.para: Get the fill color of a command.

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommand from which to get the fill color

simplesect.para: fill color of the given GDrawCommand

briefdescription.para: Return whether a command is hidden.

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommand from which to get the hidden status

simplesect.para: true if command is hidden

briefdescription.para: Get the number of points in a command.
Return whether a stroke command path is open.

simplesect.para: This only works for commands of type GDrawCommandPath and GDrawCommandPrecisePath

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommand from which to get the path open status

simplesect.para: true if the path is open

briefdescription.para: Get the value of a point in a command from the specified index.

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommand from which to get a point
The index to get the point for

simplesect.para: The point in the GDrawCommand specified by point_idx
The index must be less than the number of points

briefdescription.para: Get the radius of a circle command.

simplesect.para: this only works for commands of typeGDrawCommandCircle.

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommand from which to get the circle radius

simplesect.para: The radius in pixels if command is of type GDrawCommandCircle

briefdescription.para: Get the stroke color of a command.

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommand from which to get the stroke color

simplesect.para: The stroke color of the given GDrawCommand

briefdescription.para: Get the stroke width of a command.

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommand from which to get the stroke width

simplesect.para: The stroke width of the given GDrawCommand

briefdescription.para: Get the command type.

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommand from which to get the type

simplesect.para: The type of the given GDrawCommand

briefdescription.para: Creates a GDrawCommandImage as a copy from a given image.

parameterdescription.para: Image to copy.

simplesect.para: cloned image or NULL if the operation failed

briefdescription.para: Creates a GDrawCommandImage from the specified resource (PDC file)

parameterdescription.para: Resource containing data to load and create GDrawCommandImage from.

simplesect.para: GDrawCommandImage pointer if the resource was loaded, NULL otherwise

briefdescription.para: Deletes the GDrawCommandImage structure and frees associated data.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the image to free (delete)

briefdescription.para: Draw an image.

parameterdescription.para: The destination graphics context in which to draw
Image to draw
Offset from draw context origin to draw the image

briefdescription.para: Get size of the bounding box surrounding all draw commands in the image. This bounding box can be used to set the graphics context or layer bounds when drawing the image.

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommandImage from which to get the bounding box size

simplesect.para: bounding box size

briefdescription.para: Get the command list of the image.

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommandImage from which to get the command list

simplesect.para: command list

briefdescription.para: Set size of the bounding box surrounding all draw commands in the image. This bounding box can be used to set the graphics context or layer bounds when drawing the image.

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommandImage for which to set the bounding box size
bounding box size

briefdescription.para: Draw all commands in a command list.

parameterdescription.para: The destination graphics context in which to draw
list of commands to draw

briefdescription.para: Get the command at the specified index.

simplesect.para: the specified index must be less than the number of commands in the list

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommandList from which to get a command
index of the command to get

simplesect.para: pointer to GDrawCommand at the specified index

briefdescription.para: Get the number of commands in the list.

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommandList from which to get the number of commands

simplesect.para: number of commands in command list

briefdescription.para: Iterate over all commands in a command list.

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommandList over which to iterate
iterator callback
context pointer to be passed into the iterator callback

briefdescription.para: Creates a GDrawCommandSequence as a copy from a given sequence.

parameterdescription.para: Sequence to copy

simplesect.para: cloned sequence or NULL if the operation failed

briefdescription.para: Creates a GDrawCommandSequence from the specified resource (PDC file)

parameterdescription.para: Resource containing data to load and create GDrawCommandSequence from.

simplesect.para: GDrawCommandSequence pointer if the resource was loaded, NULL otherwise

briefdescription.para: Deletes the GDrawCommandSequence structure and frees associated data.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the sequence to destroy

briefdescription.para: Get the size of the bounding box surrounding all draw commands in the sequence. This bounding box can be used to set the graphics context or layer bounds when drawing the frames in the sequence.

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommandSequence from which to get the bounds

simplesect.para: bounding box size

briefdescription.para: Get the frame that should be shown after the specified amount of elapsed time The last frame will be returned if the elapsed time exceeds the total time.

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommandSequence from which to get the frame
elapsed time in milliseconds

simplesect.para: pointer to GDrawCommandFrame that should be displayed at the elapsed time

briefdescription.para: Get the frame at the specified index.

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommandSequence from which to get the frame
Index of frame to get

simplesect.para: pointer to GDrawCommandFrame at the specified index

briefdescription.para: Get the number of frames in the sequence.

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommandSequence from which to get the number of frames

simplesect.para: number of frames in the sequence

briefdescription.para: Get the play count of the sequence.

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommandSequence from which to get the play count

simplesect.para: play count of sequence

briefdescription.para: Get the total duration of the sequence.

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommandSequence from which to get the total duration

simplesect.para: total duration of the sequence in milliseconds

briefdescription.para: Set size of the bounding box surrounding all draw commands in the sequence. This bounding box can be used to set the graphics context or layer bounds when drawing the frames in the sequence.

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommandSequence for which to set the bounds
bounding box size

briefdescription.para: Set the play count of the sequence.

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommandSequence for which to set the play count
play count

briefdescription.para: Set the fill color of a command.

parameterdescription.para: ref DrawCommand for which to set the fill color
GColor to set for the fill

briefdescription.para: Set a command as hidden. This command will not be drawn when gdraw_command_draw is called with this command.

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommand for which to set the hidden status
true if command should be hidden

briefdescription.para: Set the path of a stroke command to be open.

simplesect.para: This only works for commands of type GDrawCommandPath and GDrawCommandPrecisePath

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommand for which to set the path open status
true if path should be hidden

briefdescription.para: Set the value of the point in a command at the specified index.

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommand for which to set the value of a point
Index of the point to set the value for
new point value to set

briefdescription.para: Set the radius of a circle command.

simplesect.para: This only works for commands of type GDrawCommandCircle

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommand from which to set the circle radius
The radius to set for the circle.

briefdescription.para: Set the stroke color of a command.

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommand for which to set the stroke color
GColor to set for the stroke

briefdescription.para: Set the stroke width of a command.

parameterdescription.para: GDrawCommand for which to set the stroke width
stroke width to set for the command

briefdescription.para: Pebble Draw Commands are a way to encode arbitrary path draw and fill calls in binary format, so that vector-like graphics can be represented on the watch.

detaileddescription.para: These draw commands can be loaded from resources, manipulated in place and drawn to the current graphics context. Each GDrawCommand can be an arbitrary path or a circle with optional fill or stroke. The stroke width and color of the stroke and fill are also encoded within the GDrawCommand. Paths can can be drawn open or closed.
All aspects of a draw command can be modified, except for the number of points in a path (a circle only has one point, the center).
Draw commands are grouped into a GDrawCommandList, which can be drawn all at once. Each individual GDrawCommand can be accessed from a GDrawCommandList for modification.

A GDrawCommandList forms the basis for GDrawCommandImage and GDrawCommandFrame objects. A GDrawCommandImage represents a static image and can be represented by the PDC file format and can be loaded as a resource.
Once you have a GDrawCommandImage loaded in memory you can draw it on the screen in a LayerUpdateProc with the gdraw_command_image_draw().
A GDrawCommandFrame represents a single frame of an animated sequence, with multiple frames making up a single GDrawCommandSequence, which can also be stored as a PDC and loaded as a resource.
To draw a GDrawCommandSequence, use the gdraw_command_sequence_get_frame_by_elapsed() to obtain the current GDrawCommandFrame and gdraw_command_frame_draw() to draw it.

Draw commands also allow access to drawing with sub-pixel precision. The points are treated as Fixed point types in the format 13.3, so that 1/8th of a pixel precision is possible. Only the points in draw commands of the type GDrawCommandTypePrecisePath will be treated as higher precision.
